accept-invitation-instruction: To accept this invitation, you will need to sign out and then sign in or create a new account using the same email address used in the invitation.

members-of-a-team: Members of a team have access to specific areas, such as a new release or a new application feature.
accept-invite: You can accept the invitation to join the team by clicking the button below.
invite-create-account: To continue, you must either create a new account or login to an existing account.
no-active-team: You do not have any active team.

delete-webhook-warning: Deleting this webhook will permanently terminate its functionality. This action cannot be undone.
webhook-create-desc: Create webhook to listen to events from app.
events-description: You can choose which events are sent to which endpoint. By default, all messages are sent to all endpoints.
kickstart-your-enterprise: Kickstart your enterprise app development with Next.js SaaS Starter Kit
delete-directory-sync-warning: Deleting a directory sync will affect all apps that use its configuration. This action cannot be undone.
create-directory-message: Automatically provision and de-provision users with your directory provider.
directory-sync-message: Directory Sync Provider will ask you for the following information to configure your SCIM app.
password-criteria: Password must include uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character
webhooks-description: Webhooks are used to send notifications to your external apps.
saml-connection-established: Single Sign-On connection is enabled for your team.
new-api-key-description: API keys allow your app to communicate with our APIs.
new-api-warning: Save this API key somewhere safe. You won't be able to see it again once you close this dialog.
no-api-key-title: You haven't created any API Keys yet
no-api-key-description: API Keys allow your app to communicate with app
revoke-api-key-confirm: Are you sure you want to revoke this API Key? This action can not be undone.
api-keys-description: API keys allow you to authenticate with the API.
remove-team-confirmation: Are you sure you want to delete the team? Deleting the team will delete all resources and data associated with the team forever.
leave-team-confirmation: Are you sure you want to leave the team? You'll lose access to all resources and data associated with the team.
no-webhook-title: You haven't created any webhook yet
sp-saml-config-description: Your Identity Provider (IdP) will ask for the following details while configuring the SAML application. Share this information with your IT administrator.
sp-acs-url: ACS (Assertion Consumer Service) URL / Single Sign-On URL / Destination URL
sp-entity-id: SP Entity ID / Identifier / Audience URI / Audience Restriction
token-not-found: The verification token is invalid or has already been used.
email-verified: Your email has been successfully verified. Thank you for confirming your account.
confirm-email-description: Please click the verification link sent to your email to activate your account.
allow-only-work-email: Please use your work email to create an account.
verify-account-expired: This verification link has expired. Please enter the email address associated with your account, and we'll send you another verification link.
verify-account-link-sent: Verification link sent. Please check your email.
homepage-title: Enterprise SaaS Starter Kit by BoxyHQ
email-address-description: The email address you use to sign in to your account.
name-appearance: This is how your name will appear in the interface. Set the first and the last name.
change-password-text: You can change your password here.
change-theme: This will change the theme of the app.
custom-avatar: Click on the avatar to upload a custom one from your files.
avatar-type: Accepted file types: .png, .jpg. Max file size: 2MB.
file-type-not-supported: File type not supported (.png or .jpg only)
confirm-your-email: Please confirm your email address before you can continue.
description-invitations: Invitations that have been sent to users but have not yet been accepted.
csc-premium-and-ultimate only: available on Premium and Ultimate plans only
rpa-created: Record of Processing Activities created successfully.
rpa-delete-description: Are you sure you want to remove Register of Procedure? Your task will not be removed.
when-you-transfer-data-outside-the-eu: When you transfer data outside the European Union:
check-whether-country-has-data-protectio: Check whether the country outside the EU to which you are transferring the data has data protection legislation and whether it is recognised as adequate by the European Commission.
a-map-of-the-world-presenting-data-prote: A map of the world presenting data protection legislation.
otherwise-provide-legal-framework-for-tr: Otherwise, you will have to provide a legal framework for your transfers to ensure data protection abroad.
recipient-description: Recipient is a natural or legal person, public authority, agency or another body to which the personal data are disclosed.
multiple-selection-possible-and-if-none-: Multiple selection possible, and if None please specify on the ticket
please-attach-relevant-documents-to-the-: Please attach relevant documents to the task.
ensure-integrity-minimize-risk: Ensure the integrity of your data assets by minimizing the risk of data loss or hacking.
measures-depend-on-sensitiveness-and-ris: The measures to be taken, whether electronic or physical, depend on the sensitiveness of the data you are processing and the risks to data subjects in the event of an incident.
various-actions-antivirus-passwords-encr: Various actions must be implemented: updating your antivirus and software, regularly changing passwords and adopting complex passwords, or encrypting your data in certain situations. In the event of loss or theft of an electronic device, it will be more difficult for a third party to access it.
multiple-selection-possible-and-if-other: Multiple selection possible, and if others please specify on the ticket
please-attach-security-certification-doc: Please attach the relevant security certification and documents to the task.
list-all-persons-who-have-access-to-the-: List all persons who have access to the data;
for-example-recruitment-it-management-pr: For example: recruitment department, IT department, management, service providers, partners, hosts, etc.
please-specify-type-of-recipient-if-not-: Please specify the type of recipient; if it is not on the list, add it in details.
write-details-if-you-select-others-or-no: Write details if you selected "Others" or if there is no suitable recipient type above.
processing-must-have-purpose: A data processing operation must have a purpose, a finality, i.e. you cannot collect or process personal data simply in case it would be useful to you one day. Each data processing operation must be assigned a purpose, which must of course be lawful and legitimate in the context of your professional activity.
processing-purpose-example: Example: You collect a lot of information from your customers, when you make a delivery, issue an invoice or offer a loyalty card. All these operations on these data represent your processing of personal data for the purpose of managing your customers.
describe-principles-to-processing: Describe the principles to processing of personal data if any.
multiple-selection-possible-for-personal: Multiple selection possible for Personal Data
please-specify-the-data-retention-period: Please specify the data retention period
retention-comments-placeholder: In some cases (payroll management), it is necessary to retain certain data for a longer period of time, depending on your legal obligations or if the data are of administrative interest (litigation).
personal-data-processing-definition: A personal data processing is an operation, or set of operations, involving personal data, whatever the method used (collection, recording, organisation, storage, adaptation, modification, extraction, consultation, use, communication by diffusion, transmission or any other form of making available, linkage).
specify-future-review-date: Specify a future date for reviewing the record
tia-delete-description: Are you sure you want to remove the Transfer Impact Assessment? Your task will not be removed.
tia-created: Record of Transfer Impact Assessment created successfully.
tia-based-on-answer-is: Based on the answers given above, the transfer is
to-be-completed-by-the-exporter: To be completed by the exporter
specify-the-data-exporters-or-the-sender: Specify the data exporter(s) or the sender
procedure-has-not-been-created-for-this-: Procedure has not been created for this issue.
specify-the-data-importers-or-the-receiv: Specify the data importer(s) or the receiver
please-describe-the-data-at-issue: Please describe the data at issue.
description-of-the-data-at-issue: Description of the data at issue
please-describe-how-data-is-transferred: Please describe how data is transferred.
description-how-the-data-is-transferred: Description how the data is transferred (e.g., remote access only)
legal-analysis-on-the-lawful-access-laws: Legal analysis on the lawful access laws of importer's country
to-be-completed-by-the-importer: To be completed by the importer
targeted-lawful-access-description: Targeted lawful access (investigation): Risk of the importer (a) receiving a search warrant or subpoena (i.e. order to produce documents) from the police, a state prosecutor or other authority investigating a potential violation of local law or development of relevance for national security7), or (b) otherwise individually becoming subject of surveillance or other lawful access measures by these authorities as part of an investigation (e.g., its phone lines and Internet connections being intercepted or its cloud provider or ISP being required to produce the importer's messages or data); only those forms of lawful access need to be considered that were found to be incompatible with EU and CH law in the legal analysis
warrants-subpoenas-description: The local authorities in principle have the right to issue to the importer or its providers warrants or subpoenas as described above for the type of data at issue
violation-local-law-description: The local authorities issue such requests regardless of whether they really believe that a violation of local law has occured or that there is threat for national security (e.g., for political reasons or as a scheme of extortion)
high-violation-local-law-description: The data subjects (of the data at issue) have a high probability of violating local laws and the data at issue would be particularly interesting to investigate these violations
high-violation-data-issue-description: The importer has a high probability of violating local laws, and the data at issue would be particularly interesting to investigate these violations
investigating-importer-description: There are other reasons why the local authorities would be interested in investigating the importer (e.g., because it is considered of relevance for national security or economic espionage by the local government) and, therefore, requesting the data at issue from the importer or its providers
past-warrant-subpoena-description: In the past 5-10 years, the importer was already required to produce the type of data at issue following such a warrant or subpoena from a local authority (or, to the importer's best knowledge, one of its providers was required to grant access to the importer's data)
data-issue-investigation-description: Based on the above and the legal analysis, does the importer have reason to believe that during the assessment period it (or one of its providers) will have to produce some of the data at issue for an investigation as described above?
non-targeted-lawful-access-description: Non-targeted lawful access (mass surveillance): Risk of the importer receiving a request of an intelligence agency or other authority to participate in the routine monitoring of communications or other data)
local-issue-warrants-description: The local authorities in principle have the right to issue to the importer warrants or subpoenas as described above for the type of data at issue
local-mass-surveillance-description: The type of the data at issue is in principle of interest for mass surveillance (e.g., because it contains large volumes of third party communications or third party communications that could be of relevance for national security purposes)
local-access-mass-surveillance-descripti: The local authorities could consider the importer to be a provider that has access to such type of data (e.g., because it is offering a corresponding service or is contracted to process such data)
local-routinely-monitor-description: There are other reasons why the local authorities would be interested requiring the importer to routinely monitor the data at issue for and on behalf of the government (e.g., because it is considered of relevance for national security or economic espionage by the local government)
pass-mass-surveillance-description: In the past 5-10 years the importer was already required to engage in mass surveillance for the local authorities
pass-mass-surveillance-connection-descri: Based on the above and the legal analysis, does the importer have reason to believe that during the assessment period it will have to produce some of the data at issue in connection with such mass surveillance?
self-reporting-to-authorities-descriptio: Self-reporting to authorities: Risk of the importer being required to self-report data to the public authorities for investigational purposes)
importer-obligation-description: The importer could in principle be subject to such a reporting obligation with regard to the type of data at issue
local-self-reporting-description: The data at issue typically contains information that is subject to local self-reporting obligations (for the purpose of permitting the local authorities to investigate a matter; self-reporting obligations as part of prudential supervision are not in-scope)
past-self-reporting-description: In the past 5-10 years the importer already self-reported data of this type to the authorities
assessment-produce-report-description: Based on the above and the legal analysis, does the importer have reason to believe that it will during the assessment period have to produce the data at issue as described above?
based-on-the-answers-transfer-is: Based on the answers given above, the transfer is
encryption-in-transit-description: Is the personal data at issue protected with adequate encryption in-transit (i.e. when transmitted)?
transfer-mechanism-description: Is the personal data at issue protected by a transfer mechanism approved by applicable data protection law (e.g., the EU Standard Contractual Clauses in case of the GDPR, approved BCR, or - in the case of an onward transfer - a back-to-back-contract in line with the EU SCC), and compliance with it and its judicial enforcement be expected, insofar permitted under the importer's law?
if-answer-is-no-enter-details-into-eu-sc: If the answer is NO please enter details into the EU Standard Contractual Clauses or similar safeguard!
based-on-legal-analysis-lawful-access-la: Based on the legal analysis above, the lawful access laws in the country of the importer are compatible with EU and CH law
lawful-access-targeted-description: … in connection with targeted lawful access (e.g., investigations by the police, state prosecutors and other authorities)
mass-surveillance-telecommunications-des: … in connection with mass surveillance of telecommunications, online services, etc. (e.g., by intelligence agencies)
if-problematic-lawful-access-derogations: If a problematic lawful access were to occur on the part of the importer as per Step 3, the transfer is nevertheless permitted if one of the derogations provided for by Art. 49 GDPR or the corresponding provisions of the CH DPA applies:
provide-reasoning-for-data-transfer-prob: Provide reasoning for data transfer probability
a-problematic-targeted-lawful-access-que: ... a problematic targeted lawful access concerning the data at issue?
a-problematic-mass-surveillance-question: ... a problematic mass surveillance involving the data at issue?
a-problematic-self-reporting-obligation-: ... a problematic self-reporting obligation (according to the Importer):
based-on-responses-does-exporter-believe: Based on the responses of the importer and the analysis done, does the exporter have reason to believe that (i) the importer will during the assessment period have to produce the data at issue) and (ii) it will be unable to justify such lawful access by way of one of the derogations of Art. 49 GDPR ...)
targeted-access-eg-investigations: (e.g., investigations by the police, state prosecutors and other authorities)
in-view-of-the-above-transfer-is: In view of the above and applicable data protection laws, the transfer is:
rm-remove-description: Are you sure you want to remove the Risk Management record from the task? Your task itself will not be removed.
risk-has-not-been-created-for-this-task: Risk has not been created for this task.
describe-how-the-risk-is-to-be-treated: Describe how the risk is to be treated.
describe-how-the-risk-is-to-be-treated-d: Describe how the risk is to be treated (e.g., controlled, avoided, transferred, or accepted).
estimate-total-cost-of-mitigating-risk: Estimate the total cost of mitigating the risk.
treatment-status-description: To what extent is the planned treatment in place? 0% means only a plan exists; 100% means the treatment is fully operational.
treated-probability-description: Enter the probability that the risk will occur after mitigation.
treated-impact-description: Enter the likely impact after mitigation. Incidents due to control failures may have higher impacts. Bold treated values if they differ from raw values.
risk-description: Describe the information security risk briefly so that people will understand what risk you are assessing.
asset-owner-description: Who is the Information Asset Owner, the person accountable if the risk treatments are inadequate, incidents occur, and the organization is adversely impacted? This person must assess and treat risks adequately.
impact-description: Describe the potential impacts in business terms if the risk occurs. Decide whether to use "worst case" or "anticipated" impacts consistently.
raw-probability-description: Enter the likelihood that the risk would occur untreated, as a percentage value.
raw-impact-description: Enter the potential business impact if the risk occurred without any treatment, as a percentage value.
delete-task-warning: Are you sure you want to delete this task?
title: Title
delete-comment-warning: Are you sure you want to delete this comment?
request-subscription-message: Provide a details of subscription request.
wise-payment-details: Payment link will be added as soon as your request is processed.
auth-limited: Too many login attempts. Please try again in 1 minute.
delete-course-confirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this course?
iap-no-data: Failed to fetch Interactive Awareness Program data.
pia-remove-description: Are you sure you want to remove Privacy Impact Assessment Risk? Your task will not be removed.
what-are-the-risks-to-privacy: What are the risks to the privacy and rights of the people whose data is being processed?
c-transparency-purpose-limitation-and-da: c) Transparency, purpose limitation and data minimization
transparency-purpose-limitation-and-data: Transparency, purpose limitation and data minimization
is-data-processing-necessary-and-proport: Is the data processing necessary, and is it proportional to the purpose?
rpa-audit-logs: Record of Processing Activities Audit Logs
desc-sso-login: Select an Identity Provider to continue with SSO
desc-signin-with-saml-sso: Your ID is the slug after the hostname.
endpoint-must-be-https: The endpoint URL must be HTTPS
email-invitation-mismatch: Your email address {{email}} does not match the email address this invitation was sent to.
scan-qr-tip: Scan this QR code to make a quick payment with Wise

unknown.label: Unknown
unknown.description: Has not even been checked yet

not-performed.label: Not Performed
not-performed.description: Complete lack of recognizable policy, procedure, control etc.

performed-informally.label: Performed Informally
performed-informally.description: Development has barely started and will require significant work to fulfill the requirements

planned.label: Planned
planned.description: Progressing nicely but not yet complete

well-defined.label: Well Defined
well-defined.description: Development is more or less complete, although detail is lacking and/or it is not yet implemented, enforced and actively supported by top management

quantitatively-controlled.label: Quantitatively Controlled
quantitatively-controlled.description: Development is complete, the process/control has been implemented and recently started operating

continuously-improving.label: Continuously Improving
continuously-improving.description: The requirement is fully satisfied, is operating fully as expected, is being actively monitored and improved, and there is substantial evidence to prove all that to the auditors
